page: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 384 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is bundled as 3,600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff. Single-core execution is a limitation, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core and memory subsystems perform significantly better. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks rank in the top 25%. Passmark CPU, encryption, and physics tests also place in the top 25%, and Redis SET operations reach a strong 55.68 million operations per second. Multi-threaded decompression and software compilation benchmarks fall into the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5ad.24xlarge leverages AMD EPYC hardware and DDR4 memory to offer high resource density and substantial local NVMe storage without the premium associated with high-frequency single-core processors. This makes it a viable option for multi-threaded workloads that can exploit the 96 vCPUs and high memory bandwidth. Recommended workloads include database operations, Redis caching layers, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose applications that require high-capacity local scratch storage but do not depend on high single-thread clock speeds.
description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 48 physical AMD EPYC 7571 cores, and 384 GB of DDR4 memory. It includes 3,600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks highlight a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and text processing are weak, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas memory bandwidth, Passmark CPU encryption, and Redis SET operations rank in the top 25%. Multi-core processing and software compilation perform at an average level. Qualitatively, the instance offers a cost-effective profile for high-density, multi-threaded workloads by utilizing AMD EPYC processors and DDR4 memory. It is best suited for database operations, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that benefit from large local NVMe storage and high memory throughput rather than raw single-core speed.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3,600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, offering strong memory bandwidth and multi-core efficiency.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge features 96 AMD vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3.6 TB NVMe SSD, delivering strong memory bandwidth for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density general-purpose platform combining ninety-six AMD vCPUs with high-capacity local NVMe storage and strong memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM on AMD EPYC 7571 architecture
3,600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong memory bandwidth and Passmark encryption performance
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded processing
Tradeoff of lower single-core performance for high resource density
